description_long: This object consists of two medium-sized, irregularly shaped stones with rounded edges and a slightly flattened profile. The stones have a predominantly gray coloration with subtle hints of green and blue, and faint white veining or mottling across their surfaces. Their texture appears hard and rough, with some areas showing minor surface abrasions and natural imperfections. The stones are positioned side by side, suggesting they may have been placed intentionally or naturally settled together. The surface finish is matte, with low reflectivity, and the stones exhibit no visible artificial modifications or carvings. These stones are typical of those found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features along sidewalks or plazas.
description: A pair of rounded, gray stones with subtle veining and a rough, matte surface, positioned side by side. Their natural texture and coloration suggest use in urban landscaping or as obstacle barriers.
description_view_0: Two stones are seen side by side, with the left stone appearing slightly smaller and more elongated than the right.
description_view_1: The stones are viewed from an angle that emphasizes their rounded tops and the subtle color variations on their surfaces.
description_view_2: Both stones are visible from above, highlighting their flattened profiles and the separation between them.
description_view_3: The stones are seen from a diagonal top-down perspective, showing their irregular shapes and the roughness of their surfaces.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or sidewalk edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with other stones on the ground surface
attribute_pattern: subtle natural veining and mottling with irregular, organic distribution
attribute_weathering: mildly weathered with some surface abrasions and a generally rough, matte finish
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with soil, grass, or gravel, often used to demarcate boundaries or as decorative obstacles in urban green spaces
